tool_calls.name: submit_final_result
tool_calls.args:
{"final_result": "## Related-Party Relationships and Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity in the SpaceX Consolidated Structure

### Overview of Consolidated Structure

Following the common-control mergers — X Holdings into xAI on March 28, 2025, and xAI into SpaceX on February 2, 2026 — the consolidated entity encompasses SpaceX, xAI, X Holdings (X), and X.AI Corp. The filings treat these entities as components of a single economic unit under Elon Musk's controlling financial interest throughout all historical periods presented. All financial statements have been retrospectively recast under common-control accounting.

---

### Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated Structure

The filing identifies several layers of related-party relationships:

**1. Elon Musk (Founder, CEO, CTO, Chairman & Principal Shareholder)**
- Controls SpaceX, xAI, and X through majority voting interest
- Also CEO, Technoking, director, and ~20% shareholder of Tesla
- Transactions with SpaceX include: security services ($2M–$4M annually, 2023–2025), aircraft usage reimbursements, and $1,421M in stock purchases in 2025

**2. Affiliated Musk Entities**
- **Musk Industries LLC**: Lease payments from xAI for office space ($0.5M in 2024, $2M in 2025)
- **The Boring Company**: Lease payments from X ($0.1M in 2024, $1M in 2025); construction services for SpaceX ($1M in 2025)
- **Craft Aviation Services, LLC**: Aircraft maintenance for SpaceX ($1M–$3M annually, 2023–2025)

**3. Antonio J. Gracias & Valor Equity Partners**
- Gracias is a SpaceX board member and founder/CEO/CIO of Valor Equity Partners
- Three major equipment (AI infrastructure hardware) lease agreements with aggregate cash payments of $6,986M, $6,633M, and $6,587M over the lease terms
- Total lease payments: $885M (2025), $857M (Jan–Feb 2026)
- Classified as **failed sale-leaseback transactions** and recorded as debt: ~$1,121M (current) and ~$7,920M (non-current) as of March 31, 2026
- API service payments from X to Valor: $1M (2024), $1M (2025)

**4. Tesla, Inc.**
- Tesla invested **$2,000M** in xAI via Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, which converted into the right to acquire 3.8 million SpaceX Class A Common Shares (pre-split) post-merger
- SpaceX purchased Tesla Megapacks ($191M in 2024, $506M in 2025, $34M in Q1 2026) and Cybertrucks ($131M in 2025)
- Tesla purchased advertising on the X platform ($4M in 2025)
- SpaceX invoiced Tesla for Musk's aircraft usage

**5. Gwynne Shotwell & Bret Johnsen**
- President/COO and CFO who co-own a personal aircraft; occasionally used for SpaceX business subject to reimbursement

---

### Where the Filing Discusses Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

**1. Note 1 — Nature of Business / Business Combinations**
- Describes the X Holdings merger into xAI (March 28, 2025): X Holdings shares converted at a 2.776:1 ratio into xAI shares; X and X.AI Corp. became wholly-owned subsidiaries of xAI
- Describes the xAI merger into SpaceX (February 2, 2026): SpaceX issued 321.7M Class A shares, 121.7M Class B shares, and $2,947M in cash to xAI stockholders
- Explains common-control accounting: net assets combined at historical carrying amounts with no goodwill recognized
- States: "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"

**2. Note 2 — Summary of Significant Accounting Policies**
- Reaffirms the broad principle: "All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation"
- Notes this constitutes a "change in reporting entity" with retrospective restatement of all periods

**3. "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" Section**
- This is the primary location for the **detailed enumeration** of specific pre-merger intercompany activity
- Itemizes all transactions between the consolidated entities and their affiliates, including commercial agreements, leases, services, and asset usage
- Reports dollar amounts by year (2023, 2024, 2025, and Jan–Feb 2026) for each category
- Threshold disclosure: transactions exceeding $120,000 since January 1, 2023
- Scope includes all transactions involving directors, executive officers, >5% stockholders, and their affiliates

**4. Note 12/13 — Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock & Shareholders' Equity**
- Details the conversion mechanics of xAI redeemable convertible preferred stock into SpaceX shares at a 0.1433 exchange ratio
- Documents Tesla's $2B xAI investment and its post-merger conversion into SpaceX equity
- Covers the full capital stock and equity adjustments from the mergers

**5. Share-Based Compensation Note**
- Details conversion of xAI and X employee stock options and RSUs into SpaceX awards using the exchange ratios (0.1433 for xAI, 2.776 for X)

---

### Key Accounting Implications

- **No separate entity financials provided**: Because of the retrospective common-control presentation, standalone financial statements for xAI and X are not available
- **Eliminated intercompany balances**: The filing does not provide a line-by-line breakdown of specific intercompany receivables/payables that were eliminated; they are netted within the combined historical figures
- **Implicit pre-merger integration**: The common-control framework implies the entities operated as part of a single economic unit prior to formal mergers, with financial activities structured as internal transfers within the Musk-controlled group

---

### Sources

| Source | URL |
|--------|-----|
| SpaceX S-1 Filing (May 20, 2026) | https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm |"}

parsed_args.final_result:
## Related-Party Relationships and Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity in the SpaceX Consolidated Structure

### Overview of Consolidated Structure

Following the common-control mergers — X Holdings into xAI on March 28, 2025, and xAI into SpaceX on February 2, 2026 — the consolidated entity encompasses SpaceX, xAI, X Holdings (X), and X.AI Corp. The filings treat these entities as components of a single economic unit under Elon Musk's controlling financial interest throughout all historical periods presented. All financial statements have been retrospectively recast under common-control accounting.

---

### Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated Structure

The filing identifies several layers of related-party relationships:

**1. Elon Musk (Founder, CEO, CTO, Chairman & Principal Shareholder)**
- Controls SpaceX, xAI, and X through majority voting interest
- Also CEO, Technoking, director, and ~20% shareholder of Tesla
- Transactions with SpaceX include: security services ($2M–$4M annually, 2023–2025), aircraft usage reimbursements, and $1,421M in stock purchases in 2025

**2. Affiliated Musk Entities**
- **Musk Industries LLC**: Lease payments from xAI for office space ($0.5M in 2024, $2M in 2025)
- **The Boring Company**: Lease payments from X ($0.1M in 2024, $1M in 2025); construction services for SpaceX ($1M in 2025)
- **Craft Aviation Services, LLC**: Aircraft maintenance for SpaceX ($1M–$3M annually, 2023–2025)

**3. Antonio J. Gracias & Valor Equity Partners**
- Gracias is a SpaceX board member and founder/CEO/CIO of Valor Equity Partners
- Three major equipment (AI infrastructure hardware) lease agreements with aggregate cash payments of $6,986M, $6,633M, and $6,587M over the lease terms
- Total lease payments: $885M (2025), $857M (Jan–Feb 2026)
- Classified as **failed sale-leaseback transactions** and recorded as debt: ~$1,121M (current) and ~$7,920M (non-current) as of March 31, 2026
- API service payments from X to Valor: $1M (2024), $1M (2025)

**4. Tesla, Inc.**
- Tesla invested **$2,000M** in xAI via Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, which converted into the right to acquire 3.8 million SpaceX Class A Common Shares (pre-split) post-merger
- SpaceX purchased Tesla Megapacks ($191M in 2024, $506M in 2025, $34M in Q1 2026) and Cybertrucks ($131M in 2025)
- Tesla purchased advertising on the X platform ($4M in 2025)
- SpaceX invoiced Tesla for Musk's aircraft usage

**5. Gwynne Shotwell & Bret Johnsen**
- President/COO and CFO who co-own a personal aircraft; occasionally used for SpaceX business subject to reimbursement

---

### Where the Filing Discusses Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

**1. Note 1 — Nature of Business / Business Combinations**
- Describes the X Holdings merger into xAI (March 28, 2025): X Holdings shares converted at a 2.776:1 ratio into xAI shares; X and X.AI Corp. became wholly-owned subsidiaries of xAI
- Describes the xAI merger into SpaceX (February 2, 2026): SpaceX issued 321.7M Class A shares, 121.7M Class B shares, and $2,947M in cash to xAI stockholders
- Explains common-control accounting: net assets combined at historical carrying amounts with no goodwill recognized
- States: "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"

**2. Note 2 — Summary of Significant Accounting Policies**
- Reaffirms the broad principle: "All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation"
- Notes this constitutes a "change in reporting entity" with retrospective restatement of all periods

**3. "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" Section**
- This is the primary location for the **detailed enumeration** of specific pre-merger intercompany activity
- Itemizes all transactions between the consolidated entities and their affiliates, including commercial agreements, leases, services, and asset usage
- Reports dollar amounts by year (2023, 2024, 2025, and Jan–Feb 2026) for each category
- Threshold disclosure: transactions exceeding $120,000 since January 1, 2023
- Scope includes all transactions involving directors, executive officers, >5% stockholders, and their affiliates

**4. Note 12/13 — Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock & Shareholders' Equity**
- Details the conversion mechanics of xAI redeemable convertible preferred stock into SpaceX shares at a 0.1433 exchange ratio
- Documents Tesla's $2B xAI investment and its post-merger conversion into SpaceX equity
- Covers the full capital stock and equity adjustments from the mergers

**5. Share-Based Compensation Note**
- Details conversion of xAI and X employee stock options and RSUs into SpaceX awards using the exchange ratios (0.1433 for xAI, 2.776 for X)

---

### Key Accounting Implications

- **No separate entity financials provided**: Because of the retrospective common-control presentation, standalone financial statements for xAI and X are not available
- **Eliminated intercompany balances**: The filing does not provide a line-by-line breakdown of specific intercompany receivables/payables that were eliminated; they are netted within the combined historical figures
- **Implicit pre-merger integration**: The common-control framework implies the entities operated as part of a single economic unit prior to formal mergers, with financial activities structured as internal transfers within the Musk-controlled group

---

### Sources

| Source | URL |
|--------|-----|
| SpaceX S-1 Filing (May 20, 2026) | https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm |
